en.purpose: To define the term downtime and pinpoint the important components of the term
en.use:
Downtime is a term commonly use by clinicians when referring to start of a cardiac arrest to return of spontaneous circulation. 
It is an important term in cardiac arrest related to patients prognosis and outcomes.
en.misuse: Not to be confused with pastime, leisure time

other_details.references:
(1) Kim WY, Giberson TA, Uber A, Berg K, Cocchi MN, Donnino MW. Neurologic outcome in comatose patients resuscitated from out-of-hospital cardiac arrest with prolonged downtime and treated with therapeutic hypothermia. Resuscitation. 2014 Aug 1;85(8):1042-6.
(2) Kim WY, Ahn S, Hong JS, Cho GC, Seo DW, Jeung KW, Kim YM, Park KN, Berg K, Donnino MW. The impact of downtime on neurologic intact survival in patients with targeted temperature management after out-of-hospital cardiac arrest: National multicenter cohort study. Resuscitation. 2016 Aug 1;105:203-8.

gt0001.description: To describe the clinical concept of the term "downtime" used by clinicians in situations of cardiac arrest.
